While the names are identical, the card record describes an individual in Port Jervis, New York, specializing in electronics. Historical business records indicate a separate 'Fantasy Unlimited' existed specifically in Port Jervis that dealt with electronic magic effects and technical components, which is a much more likely match for an electronics professional in that specific location than the large-scale horror magazine publisher.

results.possibleIdentity: Robert S. Kugler was an electronics and electrical engineer based in Port Jervis, New York, who was active during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He is identified as an electrical engineer who attended Lehigh University, appearing in the university's 1931 yearbook, 'Epitome,' as a student from Port Jervis. During the countercultural era of the late 1960s, he became associated with Fantasy Unlimited, a multimedia artists' collective and commune founded in 1967 by Peter Nevard and David Edward Byrd. Fantasy Unlimited operated from a 110-acre farm in Port Jervis and specialized in creating elaborate, psychedelic multimedia light shows for rock concerts (notably at the Fillmore East for acts like The Jimi Hendrix Experience) and corporate presentations. Kugler's role likely involved the technical design and maintenance of the collective's complex electronics, including strobe lights, liquid projectors, and slide projection systems. His collaboration with the artistic collective aligned with the goals of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, which promoted partnerships between artists and engineers to explore new technological frontiers in creative work.
